The palmerpenguins dataframe can be made available in R by calling `library(palmerpenguins)`.
The dataframe is named `penguins`.
It has the following factor columns: species, island, sex.
It has the following numeric columns: bill_length_mm, bill_depth_mm, flipper_length_mm, body_mass_g, year.
Using the ggplot2 library, construct a boxplot plot named `myplot` with species on the x-axis and bill_length_mm on the y-axis.
Do not add any additional elements to the plot.
Respond with code only; no explanation.
Do not re-iterate your objective or state anything about your reasoning process.

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(palmerpenguins)

myplot <-
    ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, y = bill_length_mm)) + 
    geom_boxplot()

right1 <-
    ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, y = bill_length_mm)) + 
    geom_boxplot()

wrong1 <-
    ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, y = bill_length_mm)) + 
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_point()

wrong2 <-
    ggplot(penguins, aes(x = species, y = bill_length_mm)) + 
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_point() +
    labs(x = "Species")

ggplot_build(myplot)$data
ggplot_build(right1)$data
ggplot_build(wrong1)$data
waldo::compare(right1@layers, myplot@layers)
all.equal(myplot@scales$'super', wrong1@scales$'super')
myplot@layers
wrong1@layers



all.equal(myplot@scales, wrong1@scales)

myplot@guides
myplot@mapping
myplot@theme
myplot@coordinates
myplot@facet
myplot@layout
myplot@labels
myplot@meta
myplot@plot_env

myplot == wrong1

class(myplot)

all(names(myplot@layers) == "geom_boxplot")

str(myplot)

str(plot)
```
